DISCOVERY, 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS -- CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 9, 2004 AT 9:00 A.M. PER MOVING PARTY. (DUPLICATE ENTRY)
REPLY TO DEFT'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA CORP.

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR OBEYING THE PREVIOUS ORDER OF THE COURT IS GRANTED. NEITHER THE DECLARATION THAT IS LEGALLY INSUFFICIENT NOR THE MATERIALS ATTACHED TO THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION EXCUSE THE LACK OF COMPLIANCE WITH THE ORDER. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,161.30, TO BE PAID ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 23, 2004. THE COURT ORDERS THAT DEFENDANT CONTACT COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 23, 2004 TO SCHEDULE THE INSPECTION AND COPYING OF THE REQUESTED RECORDS AT EITHER THE LAW OFFICE OF JOSEPH BARTON OR ANOTHER LOCATION AGREED UPON BY THE PARTIES. HEARING ON THE MOTION FOR ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 30, 2004 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 610. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SF POLICE ASSO., ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER

APPEARANCES:ROBERT D. PERKINS,ESQ. 510/337-1001 FOR PLAINTIFF-(SF POL.ASSO)-MP

RULING; Argued and the court GRANTS THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER.Prevailing party to prepare order.( DEFENDANT DAVID DERMER CALLED ADVISING THE COURT THAT HE WILL BE LATE, COMING FROM VALLEJO. AT AROUND 9:50AM THE COURT CALLED AGAIN THIS MATTER, BUT THE SAID DEFENDANT WAS NOT IN COURT YET. ANYWAY, THE COURT HEARD THE MOVING PARTY ARGUMENT AND RULED ON THE MATTER.THE DEFENDANT IN PRO PER SHOWED-UP IN COURT AT 11:10AM. AND THE COURT DID NOT ALLOW HIM TO ARGUE BECAUSE THE MATTER WAS ALREADY HEARD & RULED.HENCE, NO EX-PARTE COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE COURT AND THE SAID DEFENDANT IN PRO PER).(302/REQ/HE)BELDON YEE # 2647
